components.task_instruction:
You are tasked with classifying customer support queries related to banking or financial services into exactly one of a predefined set of support categories. Your input will be a short text (in English) from a customer describing an issue, question, or request. Your output must be the single most accurate and specific category label from the given list, and nothing else—no explanations or extra material.

## Input format

- The input will always be formatted as follows:
components.label::automatic_top_up: Problems or queries about setting up, managing, or concerns with recurring/automatic top-up features.
components.label::balance_not_updated_after_bank_tr: Customer’s balance does not show or reflect an expected incoming bank transfer.
components.label::beneficiary_not_allowed:
Inability to add, pay, or interact with a beneficiary (recipient); includes cases where the system or bank blocks beneficiary actions or shows errors related to allowed recipients.

- If an issue involves not being able to add or pay a beneficiary, choose ‘beneficiary_not_allowed’ even if a failure message is involved.

Example: If the customer says "Can someone explain why I'm getting a message on my screen saying my transfer isn't possible? I've done several in the past and haven't had a single problem until now. What's going on?", and it is determined that the system is blocking beneficiary actions, select ‘beneficiary_not_allowed’ (not ‘failed_transfer’).
components.label::cancel_transfer: Requests to stop or revoke a transfer that is already in progress.
components.label::card_payment_fee_charged:
Queries about or disputes over fees charged for making purchases or payments with a debit or credit card (not for transfers).

- When fees are mentioned, distinguish between card payment fees (card_payment_fee_charged) and transfer-related fees (transfer_fee_charged).
components.label::card_payment_not_recognised: Reports of unrecognized, suspicious, fraudulent, or mistaken card transactions (includes mistaken or unauthorized card payments).
components.label::declined_cash_withdrawal: Issues where cash withdrawal attempts (ATM or otherwise) are declined.
components.label::exchange_rate:
Issues, complaints, or queries about currency exchange rates, poor conversion, or unexpected rate application, especially relating to loss due to rate.

- For statements about a recipient getting less than sent, use ‘transfer_fee_charged’ unless there's an explicit complaint about currency exchange rates (then use ‘exchange_rate’).
components.label::exchange_via_app: Using the banking app or mobile application to perform a currency exchange and facing issues/questions regarding this process.
components.label::failed_transfer: Any type of transfer that did not complete—system or bank indicates the transfer failed generally (unless otherwise specified as pending, declined, or beneficiary-related).
components.label::get_disposable_virtual_card:
Requests about or issues receiving, accessing, or using a one-time disposable virtual card.

- If about virtual/disposable cards, use ‘get_disposable_virtual_card’.
components.label::get_physical_card:
Requests about receiving, ordering, or waiting for a physical (plastic) card; includes delivery delays or cards lost in transit.

- If customer mentions waiting for or not receiving a physical card, choose ‘get_physical_card’.
components.label::passcode_forgotten:
Customer forgets or needs to reset their account passcode or PIN (does not include issues about card delivery or activation).

- For “forgotten PINs or passcodes”, always use ‘passcode_forgotten’.
components.label::pending_top_up: Adding funds is pending or incomplete; the top-up process has started but is not finished.
components.label::pending_transfer: A funds transfer that is not yet completed and remains in a pending state.
components.label::receiving_money: Customer needs information on how to receive funds, if receiving is possible, or when an incoming transfer is expected.
components.label::reverted_card_payment?:
The customer is asking about a card payment that shows as cancelled, refunded, or reverted.

- For cancelled or reversed card payments, choose ‘reverted_card_payment?’.

Example: If a user says, "My card payment has been cancelled," the correct label is ‘reverted_card_payment?’ (not ‘card_payment_not_recognised’), as the customer is reporting a payment that has been cancelled or reversed.
components.label::top_up_by_cash_or_cheque: Customer issues or questions about adding funds to their account specifically by cash or cheque (not card or transfer).
components.label::top_up_reverted: A top-up transaction (any funding method) that is refunded, failed and returned, or otherwise reverted.
components.label::topping_up_by_card: Issues or queries about adding funds specifically by credit or debit card.
components.label::transfer_fee_charged:
Fees charged for sending or receiving money transfers (NOT card payment fees). If the customer says a recipient got less than sent without mentioning exchange rates, use this label.

- When fees are mentioned, distinguish between card payment fees (card_payment_fee_charged) and transfer-related fees (transfer_fee_charged).
- For statements about a recipient getting less than sent, use ‘transfer_fee_charged’ unless there's an explicit complaint about currency exchange rates (then use ‘exchange_rate’).
components.label::transfer_into_account: Issues or requests about depositing/putting money into the customer’s own account (general incoming transfers).
components.label::transfer_timing: Questions about transfer processing times, how long it will take, speed, or timing concerns for transfer completion.
components.label::verify_my_identity: Customer needs assistance with verifying identity or encountering issues with ID/document checks during onboarding or transactions.
components.label::verify_source_of_funds: Customer is being asked to prove or provide information about where their funds came from (source of funds validation).
components.boundary_rules:
- Classify *only* based on the explicit content of the user's statement—do not infer intent or context beyond what is written.
- Choose the most specific category relevant to the customer’s actual reported problem or direct request; never default to generic categories.
- When fees are mentioned, distinguish between card payment fees (card_payment_fee_charged) and transfer-related fees (transfer_fee_charged).
- For statements about a recipient getting less than sent, use ‘transfer_fee_charged’ unless there's an explicit complaint about currency exchange rates (then use ‘exchange_rate’).
- If customer mentions waiting for or not receiving a physical card, choose ‘get_physical_card’. If about virtual/disposable cards, use ‘get_disposable_virtual_card’.
- For cancelled or reversed card payments, choose ‘reverted_card_payment?’.
- If an issue involves not being able to add or pay a beneficiary, choose ‘beneficiary_not_allowed’ even if a failure message is involved.
- For “forgotten PINs or passcodes”, always use ‘passcode_forgotten’.
- Always select just *one* category per query.
